outbound_strategy.reply_style: Short, genuine compliments referencing specific visual elements (lighting, expression, composition). Use question-based engagement when appropriate. Keep replies to 1-2 sentences, avoid generic praise. Match the casual, conversational tone of top EN performers.

ab_test.variant_a: 3 posts per day at slots 1-3 only
ab_test.variant_b: 4 posts per day using all 4 slots

key_insights: EN market reply-adjusted avg ER is 6.1% — sessypuuh leads at 38.1% with only 0.7 posts/day and zero hashtags, confirming that quality over quantity drives engagement in this niche

Viral content pattern is clear: leahyunaxo's top posts (16.6K, 15.5K, 9.0K likes) all use short question-based captions like 'wifey material?', 'be honest.. am I attractive?', 'describe me in 1 word?' paired with a single AI beauty image — adopt this engagement-bait caption style for high-priority portrait slots

85.7% of fetched competitors (12 of 14) use zero hashtags. The hashtag signal analysis shows 'strong_negative' correlation between hashtag usage and engagement rate — reduce max_per_post from 4 to 2, focusing on niche-specific #AIart only as a consistent tag

IvoryLane_plus (253K followers) uses question-based captions ('this ok?', 'Too big or just right?', 'say hi if you see this') with 1 post/day at a consistent 15:00 UTC schedule, achieving 0.9% ER — consistent timing + interactive captions is a proven mid-scale growth formula

Impression-based ER diverges significantly from follower-based ER: sessypuuh shows 9.4% impression-based vs 38.1% follower-based, indicating high algorithmic reach relative to follower count — focus on producing the type of content that gets recommended beyond follower base
risks: The posting frequency A/B test (day 3 of 5) is still running — maintain both variants consistently through 2026-03-07 before drawing conclusions. Prematurely changing frequency would invalidate the test.

27 of 41 tracked competitors returned 'not_found' errors (65.9% skip rate), including previously high-value targets @baharaykin, @yuumispm, @Ava_starz — these may be suspended or renamed. Investigate and update competitors.json before next pipeline run.

Top EN performers have 7K-1.2M followers — breaking through requires differentiated visual style since question-based captions are becoming saturated across multiple accounts (leahyunaxo, sessypuuh, Angelwithcakee, yogana_19 all use variations)

outbound_strategy.reply_style: Polite and warm in Japanese — use casual keigo, light emoji (1-2 per reply), and specific compliments about artwork detail, color choice, or character expression. Match the supportive peer-community tone of the JP AI art space.

key_insights: JP data is severely limited — only 1 JP-market competitor (PinUpGirl_AI, market=both) was fetched today, with 27 JP competitors unreachable due to API errors. All JP strategy recommendations should be treated as provisional until data coverage improves.

PinUpGirl_AI (66K followers, 10 posts/day, 0.018% raw ER, 0.04% reply-adjusted) demonstrates that high-volume posting with 60% reply contamination yields near-zero engagement — reinforces the quality-over-quantity approach for JP (keep to 3-4 posts/day max)

JP hashtag strategy diverges from EN: since JP competitors that were previously tracked (tororo_223, ai_nontan_room) used #AI美女 and #AIイラスト as discovery tags, maintain these for JP even though EN data suggests hashtags are unnecessary — JP X culture relies more on hashtag-based discovery than EN

The hashtag count A/B test (day 3 of 5) continues — JP Variant A (3 tags) vs Variant B (5 tags) will provide critical data on whether JP audience responds to heavier tagging. Do not change the test mid-run.

Cross-market opportunity: ai_hiyo_ (387K followers, market=EN) posts in both English and Japanese, achieving 0.4% ER with 10 posts/day. Their Grok image-editing engagement mechanic ('hey @grok tap the post to combine those four images') drove 729 likes — interactive AI tool integration is a differentiator worth testing in JP
risks: With 27 JP competitors unreachable, the JP strategy is effectively flying blind — set conservative expectations and focus on building a baseline content library rather than optimizing for engagement patterns we cannot reliably measure

PinUpGirl_AI is the only JP data point and it has a market='both' classification with Chinese-language content mixed in — it may not be representative of the core JP AI beauty audience. Avoid over-indexing on this single data point.

Previous JP outbound targets (@tororo_223, @ai_nontan_room, @yunaelegant, @AI_MIKENEKO) are all now unreachable — pivot outbound to reachable accounts that post in Japanese (ai_hiyo_, NotjustRen00 who posts in Chinese but has JP audience overlap)
